governing_bodies.name: Austin City Council

your_representative.name: Natasha Harper-Madison
your_representative.note: Term-limited Jan 2027. Open seat election Nov 3, 2026. Current candidates: Steven Brown, Alexandria Anderson.
your_representative.alignment_notes: Generally supportive of housing development and equity initiatives

governing_bodies.meeting_schedule: Thursdays, 10am, Austin City Hall, 301 W 2nd St
governing_bodies.public_comment_process: Sign up online at austintexas.gov/speakup by noon the day before, or register in person. 3 minutes per speaker. Remote participation available.

governing_bodies.name: Travis County Commissioners Court

your_representative.name: Margaret Gómez (current) — Runoff pending
your_representative.note: May 26, 2026 Democratic runoff for this seat. Candidates: Susanna Ledesma Morales (leading), opponent TBD. Low-turnout runoff = high individual vote impact.

governing_bodies.public_comment_process: Online registration opens 8pm two nights before meeting, closes 8am day-of. Remote participation by phone. In-person also available.

upcoming_elections.race: Travis County Commissioner Precinct 4 — Democratic Runoff
upcoming_elections.relevance: Directly represents your precinct. Very low expected turnout = outsized individual vote impact. HIGH PRIORITY.
upcoming_elections.action: Verify voter registration. Research candidates. Consider volunteering — small canvassing effort could shift outcome.

upcoming_elections.relevance: Your city council district. Harper-Madison is term-limited. Open seat = rare opportunity to influence who represents you. Filing deadline: Aug 17, 2026.
upcoming_elections.action: Monitor candidate announcements. Evaluate alignment with your priorities. Consider early engagement/donation to preferred candidate.

key_political_dynamics: Austin politics center on a housing/growth vs. neighborhood preservation tension. The current council leans pro-development with Mayor Watson providing a pragmatic anchor, but faces organized NIMBY opposition on specific projects. The Nov 2026 city council elections (Districts 1, 3, 5, 8, 9) will shape Austin's direction on housing for the next 4 years. District 1 is the most consequential for 78702 residents — it's an open seat for the first time in 8 years. Travis County is solidly blue; meaningful political competition is within the Democratic primary. The May 26 Pct 4 runoff is the next actionable election.
